chapter_title.en: The Chapter on the Virtues of Prayer

text: 629 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا يَقُومُ إِلَى اَلصَّلاَةِ إِلاَّ اِكْتَنَفَتْهُ بِعَدَدِ مَنْ خَالَفَهُ مَلاَئِكَةٌ يُصَلُّونَ خَلْفَهُ وَ يَدْعُونَ اَللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَهُ حَتَّى يَفْرُغَ مِنْ صَلاَتِهِ»

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.629 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"There is no servant from among our followers (Shiite) who stands for prayer except that an equal number of angels, matching those who oppose him, surround him and pray behind him, invoking Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, for him until he completes his prayer."
